title: Views, Triggers, Reusable Database Logic, and Boundaries · SQL Query Room
summary: Multiple applications need a stable read contract and audited state transitions without hidden business behavior. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a versioned read model and audited transition design while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy.
Choose live views, materialized views, summary tables, and cache refresh strategies from freshness needs.
Predict trigger timing, row versus statement scope, recursion, ordering limits, and failure behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop View Contracts
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Publish a stable open_ticket_queue view and audit status changes for ticket 103. Expose no private member fields, prevent recursive trigger behavior, and prove rollback removes both visible and audit effects.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop View Contracts.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for design views as named query contracts with explicit columns, ownership, and change policy. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop View Contracts, keep View Contracts connected to executable relational evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Trigger Audit Test
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-trigger-audit-test-1ydcmy" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; view, trigger, reusable-logic, or side-effect evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Trigger Audit Test.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Trigger Audit Test into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Trigger Audit Test, keep Trigger Audit Test connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-trigger-audit-test-1ydcmy
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
CREATE TEMP TRIGGER audit_status_1ydcmy
AFTER UPDATE OF status ON tickets
BEGIN
  SELECT CASE WHEN NEW.status = OLD.status THEN RAISE(IGNORE) END;
END;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Trigger Audit Test structural check is only one gate. a versioned read model and audited transition design must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
